steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Study 1 establishes that genetic similarity between breeds explains behavioral similarity in trainability. Study 2 finds bulldogs and French bulldogs show similar trainability. The question asks what logically follows from combining both findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comparison to breeds not in Study 2: Introduces breeds not discussed — unsupported by the evidence. ✗
  • Choice B — contradicts the similarity finding: Study 2 shows similar trainability, not dissimilar. ✗
  • Choice C — shared genetic factors explain similar trainability: Directly applies Study 1's framework to Study 2's finding — similar trainability suggests shared genetics. ✓
  • Choice D — speculates about future change: Neither study addresses future change. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
